claim: autoMemoryDirectory pointed at the git repo's .claude/canonical-memory/ directory is the lowest-friction cross-locale sync solution for SoNash's two-Windows-different-username scenario.

autoMemoryDirectory can only be set in settings.local.json or user-level settings, not in .claude/settings.json. This is a deliberate security restriction, not a bug, and requires per-locale manual configuration.
The .claude/canonical-memory/ directory is git-tracked and contains 20 files but is diverged from live auto-memory by at least 7 feedback entries and contains an incorrect user expertise description.

Claude Code keys auto memory to absolute filesystem paths. Two machines with different usernames but the same git repo have different memory keys and cannot access each other's auto memory without explicit intervention.
Sharing a SQLite database file via OneDrive, Dropbox, or any cloud sync service risks data corruption. SQLite explicitly documents this as unsupported. WAL mode makes the risk worse, not better.

Both claude-sync tools (tawanorg and renefichtmueller) sync ~/.claude/projects/ as-is and do not remap locale-specific path keys. They do not solve the path-key problem for different-username locales.
memoir (camgitt) claims Windows path remapping 'just works' during restore, but the mechanism is entirely undocumented. No source confirms it handles two Windows machines with different usernames.
claude-brain explicitly does not support Windows native; it requires WSL.
CCMS (miwidot) is a bash script requiring Unix or WSL. It is not viable on a no-admin Windows environment without WSL.
autoMemoryDirectory was added to Claude Code in v2.1.74 (March 12, 2026) specifically to support the use case of pointing memory to OneDrive/Dropbox/git-repo paths.
The git-notes pattern solves the path-key problem by keying memory to commit hashes instead of filesystem paths, but tooling is immature and requires Python plus a vector embedding stack.
Syncthing runs as a single portable binary on Windows with no admin required. Corporate firewalls at the work locale may block Syncthing relay servers, making home-to-work sync unreliable.
codebase-memory-mcp fills a gap that no session memory system addresses: a structural AST-based code index. Pre-built Windows amd64 binary; zero dependencies; 66 languages.

The cursor-memory-bank 4-file pattern (activeContext, productContext, progress, decisionLog) converged independently across Roo-Code, Cline, and cursor-memory-bank. It is a de facto community standard for project-scoped markdown memory.

Hook-based capture (CP-1) is the only mechanism that guarantees memory writes without requiring Claude to decide to call a store tool. Supermemory explicitly rebuilt its architecture away from MCP tools to hooks for this reason.

SoNash's existing 25-hook, 14-mechanism architecture already implements hook-based capture comprehensively. The architectural gap is not coverage but queryability: captured data is not well-exposed through a retrieval layer.
The mcp__memory knowledge graph server is configured and permitted but underused. The /checkpoint --mcp skill documents its intended use. The gap is discipline, not infrastructure.
OMEGA Memory requires WSL2 on Windows with no confirmed native Windows path. This is a hard blocker at the no-admin work locale.
claude-mem has multiple documented Windows-specific failures: AbortSignal crash, pipe mode breakage, PowerShell dependency. It is rated FRAGILE on Windows.
CLAUDE.md adherence degrades when the file exceeds 200 lines. The 135-line target in SoNash's CLAUDE.md is well-reasoned and should not be allowed to grow.

The A-MAC academic finding on 'content type prior' means the most effective memory admission gate is a whitelist of category types rather than a scoring formula. Categories: user-corrections, architecture-decisions, hook-patterns, recurring-errors.

Episodic memory (timestamped events) is the weakest layer in SoNash's current architecture. The mcp__memory server stores entities/relations but not timestamped events. MEMORY.md mixes episodic and semantic content with no temporal binding.

The MEMORY.md index to individual files pattern already partially implements progressive disclosure. Completing it requires only adding an instruction in MEMORY.md for Claude to request specific topic files. No infrastructure change needed.
Auto Dream is server-gated (feature flag tengu_onyx_plover, enabled: false) and not GA as of March 2026. The community dream-skill plugin replicates the 4-phase cycle and is available now.
OMEGA Memory and Hindsight both claim top LongMemEval rankings simultaneously. These cannot both be current. Benchmark claims from either system should be treated as indicative only.

The episodic-memory (superpowers) plugin is already configured and Windows-compatible via fnm. The show tool is not in the allow-list. Validate this existing tool before adding any new vector search infrastructure.

A type-to-decay mapping for SoNash memory files: in-progress work 7 days; session observations 30 days; bug resolutions 90 days; architecture decisions, user corrections, project constraints permanent.
Adding metadata headers (last_reviewed, type, expires) to the 39 individual memory files enables a decay-checking script without any new infrastructure. This is the lowest-cost LP-2 implementation.
The learning system has automatic consolidation (every 10 PR reviews to CODE_PATTERNS.md). Session memory has no equivalent pipeline. This is the highest-priority lifecycle gap.

Signal keyword extraction from claude-supermemory — only writing MEMORY.md entries on turns containing 'decision', 'bug', 'architecture', 'correction', 'never again' — is adoptable without adopting that system.

The '2+ projects before promotion' rule from lin-yuchen is directly applicable as a governance principle for SoNash's project vs. user memory distinction. No current rules prevent project-specific observations from staying project-scoped.
Phase-based rule loading achieves approximately 70% token reduction according to the cursor-memory-bank implementation. This pattern is adoptable without installing cursor-memory-bank.

SoNash already implicitly operates as a multi-layer memory system: CLAUDE.md (always-inject) to MEMORY.md index (session-start inject) to JSONL state files (audit trail) to mcp__memory graph (on-demand retrieval). The layers exist; explicit tier boundaries and promotion rules do not.
context-sync (Intina47) is local-only. Its SQLite database has no built-in cross-machine push/pull mechanism. Wave 1 research description of it as cross-machine was incorrect.

OneDrive creates a duplicate file with a machine name suffix on concurrent writes to plain markdown files. For a solo operator who never runs both machines simultaneously, this is detectable and recoverable — not silent data corruption.
Pointing autoMemoryDirectory at a git-tracked directory has no publicly documented community implementation. It is the highest-signal sync finding but is untested in production.
doobidoo/mcp-memory-service has a Cloudflare Workers backend enabling true cross-locale sync via remote MCP. It is a Phase 2 option. Docker is required for local install on Windows without admin.

memoir requires npm install -g. This needs no admin if Node.js was installed with nvm-windows, but requires admin with a system-level Node.js install. The work locale's Node.js install method is unconfirmed.

The three highest-priority architectural gaps: (1) canonical-memory divergence + autoMemoryDirectory activation; (2) session memory consolidation pipeline; (3) memory decay metadata for 39 individual files. All three addressable without new infrastructure.

Confidence decay rates across cognitive memory types are consistent across OpenMemory and yuvalsuede implementations: emotional/progress context is most volatile; reflective/architectural content is least volatile.
The supermemory cloud plugin has an active Windows stdin bug (GitHub Issue #25, open as of February 2026). It is not recommended for this environment until confirmed fixed.
Engram is a single Go binary with MIT license, explicit Claude Code plugin marketplace support, and SQLite+FTS5. No Windows compatibility issues documented. No admin or Docker required.

The community dream-skill plugin replicates AutoDream's 4-phase consolidation cycle as a manually-triggered skill. It is available now and is the most accessible implementation of session memory consolidation.

claude-mem's PostToolUse hooks broke for 2+ months (November 2025 through January 2026) when Claude Code changed its runtime internals. This demonstrates the hook maintenance burden for third-party hook-dependent systems.
OneDrive availability at the work locale (jbell) is unconfirmed. If unavailable, the recommended sync alternative is git-only (canonical-memory + autoMemoryDirectory) or Syncthing portable binary.

mcp-memory-keeper is confirmed Windows-compatible via npx with no admin required. Its git branch-derived topic channels align with SoNash's multi-branch workflow. The minimal profile should be used to reduce token overhead.
